16	/**
17	 * Cache this `"use cache"` for a timespan defined by the `"default"` profile.
18	 * ```
19	 *   stale:      300 seconds (5 minutes)
20	 *   revalidate: 900 seconds (15 minutes)
21	 *   expire:     never
22	 * ```
23	 *
24	 * This cache may be stale on clients for 5 minutes before checking with the server.
25	 * If the server receives a new request after 15 minutes, start revalidating new values in the background.

1 import { unstable_cache } from "next/cache"; 2 import { revalidateTag } from "next/cache"; 3 import { getAllProducts } from "@/lib/db"; 4 5 /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */ 6 /* Cached product fetcher */ 7 /* */ 8 /* The `"products"` cache tag is used consistently so that a single */ 9 /* revalidation call (via syncCatalog) refreshes every view that */ 10 /* depends on catalog data. */ 11 /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */ 12 const getCachedProducts = unstable_cache( 13 async () => { 14 const products = await getAllProducts(); 15 return { 16 products, 17 fetchedAt: new Date().toISOString(), 18 }; 19 }, 20 ["admin-products"], 21 { 22 tags: ["products"], 23 /** Keep the cache forever unless explicitly invalidated — the 24 * admin's "Sync latest catalog" action is the only thing that 25 * should trigger a refresh. */ 26 revalidate: false, 27 } 28 ); 29 30 /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */ 31 /* Sync Server Action (inline) */ 32 /* */ 33 /* Invalidate all cached data tagged `"products"`, then return */ 34 /* immediately — the product list may be stale for a moment while */ 35 /* fresh data is fetched on the next page render. */ 36 /* */ 37 /* In a production system this is where you'd also enqueue an */ 38 /* ERP/PIM import job (e.g. write to a Bull/SQS queue) that runs */ 39 /* asynchronously and calls revalidateTag("products") when finished. */ 40 /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */ 41 async function syncCatalog(formData: FormData) { 42 "use server"; 48 49 revalidateTag("products", "max"); 50 } 51 52 /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */ 53 /* Page */ 54 /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */ 55 export default async function AdminProductsPage() { 56 const { products, fetchedAt } = await getCachedProducts(); attributes.io.input: {"query":"admin product catalog caching sync server action page"}
attributes.io.output:
{"results":[{"anchor_id":"repo://legacy","anchor_kind":"repo","content":"Admin product catalog page uses inline Server Action ('use server' inside page component) for "Sync latest catalog" trigger. Architecture: unstable_cache wraps getAllProducts() with tags: ['products'] for fast read-heavy browsing. The Server Action calls revalidateTag('products', 'default') inline for immediate cache invalidation, then fire-and-forgets syncCatalogFromERP() so admin gets instant response while the ~3s sync updates the in-memory store in background. Page uses export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic' to ensure re-renders after cache invalidation (preventing Full Route Cache from serving stale static HTML). The "products" cache tag is used consistently across both caching and invalidation, so any view depending on the same tag gets refreshed data after sync completes. Brief staleness between sync trigger and background completion is by design for this read-heavy admin handling thousands of SKUs.

Why: Inline Server Action keeps the sync trigger co-located with the page, no separate actions file needed. Fire-and-forget gives operators instant UX feedback while the ERP/PIM sync runs. Cache-tag model means revalidation is not coupled to any single route — all views using the "products" tag see fresh data eventually. The force-dynamic export prevents stale HTML after revalidation, which is a known pitfall (confirmed in prior session drawer_workspace_server_actions_15721b57). revalidateTag('products', 'default') uses the required second profile argument per Next.js 16.2.0-canary.41 API (confirmed in prior session drawer_workspace_server_actions_0ad6eca9).
